title: Mistä sä tulet?
subtitle: Where have you been / where are you coming from?

blocks.text: You want to ask your Finnish friend where they've been. They answer: Minä olen tulossa suomen kielen kurssilta. It sounds like a mouthful — but it's three logical pieces, and you already own the first one. Let's pull it apart.

blocks.title: Missä vs Mistä — notice the difference
blocks.text: You already know mistä from Lesson 3 (where are you from?). Missä means 'where at' — a place you are. Mistä means 'from where' — a place you're moving away from. Finnish encodes movement vs stillness right inside the question word.

blocks.title: You just learned a system, not just words
blocks.text: These three question words are the same root with three different endings: -ssä (at), -stä (from), -hin (to). Every Finnish place word works the same way. This is the skeleton of the whole location system.

blocks.translation: I have been at the course.

blocks.title: Olen ollut = I have been
blocks.text: Olen is the formal/textbook 'I am' (colloquial: oon). Ollut is the past participle of olla — to be. Together: olen ollut = I have been. This is Finnish present perfect. You'll hear it constantly.

rows: I am on my way / coming

blocks.text: Kurssilla vs kurssilta — spot the difference

blocks.content: This is the same -lla/-lta pattern as the location system above, but for surfaces and events. {Kurssilla|grammar|adessive|at/on the course} = you are there. {Kurssilta|grammar|ablative|from the course} = you are leaving from there.

items.en: I have been at the course.
I am coming from the course.

blocks.title: The -lla / -lta pair
blocks.text: -lla means 'at/on' (you are there). -lta means 'from' (you are leaving). It's the same logic as missä/mistä. Finnish is consistent — once you see the pattern, it works everywhere.

blocks.annotated: {Minä|word|pronoun|I (formal)} {olen|grammar|verb|am} {tulossa|word|verb form|coming / on my way} {suomen kielen|phrase|genitive|of Finnish language} {kurssilta|grammar|ablative|from the course}.
blocks.translation: I am coming from a Finnish language course.

blocks.title: Tulossa — a beautiful Finnish form
blocks.text: Tulossa literally means 'in a coming' — it's the verb tulla (to come) frozen mid-action. Olen tulossa = I am in the process of coming / I'm on my way. You'll use this constantly: Olen tulossa! = I'm on my way!

blocks.title: Suomen kielen kurssi
blocks.text: Suomen kieli = Finnish language. Suomen kielen = of the Finnish language (genitive case — it modifies kurssi). So suomen kielen kurssi = a course of the Finnish language. The fact that you're taking one is why you're reading this.

lines.speaker: B
lines.fi: Mä oon ollu suomen kielen kurssilla. Entä sä?
lines.en: I've been at a Finnish course. What about you?

lines.speaker: A
lines.fi: Mä tulin töistä. Mistä sä tuut nyt?
lines.en: I came from work. Where are you coming from now?

blocks.title: Free words from the dialogue
blocks.text: Siistiä = cool / neat (very colloquial, very Finnish). Joo = yeah (colloquial kyllä). Tosi = really / very (tosi hyvä = really good). Menee hyvin = it's going well (menee = it goes).

blocks.content: Ollut: OL-lut (double l, short u). Tulossa: TOO-los-sa (long u, double s). Kurssilta: KURS-sil-ta (the double s is a real stop — don't swallow it). Suomen: SOO-men (long u, clean syllables as always).

blocks.title: What you now own
items: mihin — to where (direction) — spotted the system!
olen ollut / mä oon ollu — I have been
olen tulossa — I'm on my way / coming
-lla vs -lta: at a place vs from a place
suomen kielen kurssi — Finnish language course
tosi, joo, siistiä — real colloquial filler words
